body.p:
Schemas without standards would only be a means to check the syntactic and structural correctness of XML data - in a company-wide context.
UWE test footnote (13/40, callout M): DITA fn maps to UWE footnote/key/desc.
If, however, the data is to be communicated across company boundaries, it is advisable to align the information architecture or schemas with a standard that is also readily accessible to others.
As practice shows, however, there are always some application-specific exceptions that cannot be covered by a standard.
Therefore, it is important to ensure that the standard can be extended for application-specific needs without falsifying the standardized information.
